title: Security (Common Criteria)
summary: Information and systems are protected against unauthorized access, unauthorized disclosure of information, and damage to systems that could compromise the availability, integrity, confidentiality, and privacy of information or systems.
requirements: Implement logical and physical access controls.
Conduct regular risk assessments and vulnerability management.
Establish an incident response plan and perform regular testing.
Ensure employee onboarding includes security training and background checks.
commonGaps: Lack of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for administrative access.
No formal process for revoking access when employees leave.
Missing vendor risk management program (monitoring third-party security).

title: Availability
summary: Information and systems are available for operation and use to meet the entity's objectives.
requirements: Maintain historical system performance data and capacity planning.
Implement robust disaster recovery and business continuity plans.
Use redundant infrastructure to prevent single points of failure.
Monitor system health and establish alerting for downtime.
commonGaps: Disaster recovery plans have never been tested.
Lack of real-time monitoring and alerting systems.
No documented RTO (Recovery Time Objective) or RPO (Recovery Point Objective).

title: Processing Integrity
summary: System processing is complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized to meet the entity's objectives.
requirements: Implement comprehensive quality assurance and testing procedures before deployment.
Maintain detailed audit logs of transaction processing.
Identify and correct processing errors in a timely manner.
commonGaps: Developers have direct write access to production databases.
No separation of duties between development and production deployment.
Lack of input validation leading to corrupted data states.

title: Confidentiality
summary: Information designated as confidential is protected to meet the entity's objectives.
requirements: Identify and classify all confidential information.
Implement data loss prevention (DLP) tools and policies.
Ensure secure data disposal when retention periods end.
commonGaps: Confidential client data stored locally on unencrypted laptops.
Sharing confidential data across unauthorized communication channels (e.g., Slack without oversight).

title: Privacy
summary: Personal information is collected, used, retained, disclosed, and disposed of to meet the entity's objectives.
requirements: Provide clear notice to individuals about privacy practices.
Obtain explicit consent for data collection when required.
Allow individuals to access, correct, or delete their personal information.
commonGaps: Privacy policy does not accurately reflect actual data collection practices.
No established mechanism for users to request data deletion.
Sharing data with third-parties without disclosing it in the privacy notice.
